evals.name: ipc-contract-testing-with-preload-validation
evals.description: Validate that the skill produces typed IPC channel tests, preload script validation, and main process handler tests for an Electron file manager application.
evals.input: Write a comprehensive test suite for an Electron file manager application. The app uses IPC to communicate between the renderer and main process for file operations: readDirectory, readFile, writeFile, deleteFile, and watchDirectory. Test the IPC handlers in the main process with mocked file system operations. Test the preload script to verify only the intended API is exposed via contextBridge. Validate the typed IPC channel contract between main and renderer. Use Vitest as the test framework. Ensure tests verify contextIsolation and sandbox security settings.

assertions.description: Tests must directly test ipcMain.handle registrations to verify that IPC handlers correctly process messages, validate arguments, and return expected responses without requiring full application startup.
Preload script tests must verify that contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld only exposes the intended API surface, preventing accidental exposure of Node.js APIs to the renderer process.
Tests must assert that BrowserWindow webPreferences include contextIsolation: true, verifying the security boundary that prevents renderer scripts from accessing the preload script's scope.

The IPC contract tests must cover ipcRenderer.invoke calls matched to ipcMain.handle registrations, verifying that channel names, argument types, and return types are consistent across the process boundary.
File system operations must be mocked in IPC handler tests to isolate the handler logic from the actual file system, enabling fast and deterministic test execution in CI.

evals.name: e2e-packaging-and-native-module-validation
evals.description: Validate that the skill produces end-to-end tests, packaging verification, and native module compatibility tests for an Electron application with SQLite and auto-update.
evals.input: Create a test suite for an Electron note-taking app that uses better-sqlite3 for local storage and electron-updater for auto-updates. Write: (1) E2E tests using Playwright that launch the Electron app, create a note, verify it persists across app restart, and measure startup time. (2) Packaging tests that build the app with electron-builder and verify the distributable contains correct native module binaries, has proper code signing metadata, and includes all required assets. (3) Auto-update tests that mock the update server and verify the download-verify-install lifecycle. Target macOS, Windows, and Linux.

assertions.description: E2E tests must use Playwright's Electron integration via _electron.launch() to start and interact with the actual Electron application, testing the full process architecture.
Packaging tests must verify electron-builder output to catch silently excluded files, wrong native module ABIs, or broken code signatures before distribution.

Tests must validate that better-sqlite3 native bindings are correctly compiled and included in the packaged application, since native module ABI mismatches are the most common cause of packaged app crashes.
Auto-update tests must mock electron-updater's autoUpdater to verify the update lifecycle (check, download, verify, quit-and-install) without depending on a real update server.
Tests must include platform-conditional logic or separate test suites for macOS, Windows, and Linux to account for platform-specific packaging formats (DMG, NSIS, AppImage) and behaviors.
